content:
Introduction-

2 min read
2 hours ago

--

• I recently completed my Web Development and Designing Internship at Oasis Infobyte (OIB-SIP). This internship gave me an opportunity to improve my front-end web development skills by building practical projects using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Each task challenged me to think logically, write clean code, and create responsive user interfaces.
Projects Completed -
1. Calculator
The first project was a fully functional calculator. It supports basic arithmetic operations, decimal numbers, clear and delete functions, and handles invalid inputs such as division by zero. While building this project, I gained a better understanding of JavaScript event handling and DOM manipulation.
2. Tribute Page
The second task was to design a tribute page dedicated to Ada Lovelace, one of the pioneers of computer programming. I focused on creating a responsive layout with an attractive design, biography, timeline, and inspirational quote. This project strengthened my HTML structuring and CSS styling skills.
3. To-Do Web Application
The To-Do application allows users to add, edit, complete, and delete tasks. I also implemented localStorage so tasks remain available even after refreshing the browser. This project improved my understanding of JavaScript logic, data persistence, and user interaction.
4. Login Authentication System
The final project was a login authentication system with user registration, secure password hashing, login validation, protected dashboard access, and logout functionality. Building this project helped me understand authentication concepts, session management, and browser storage.
Technologies Used-
HTML5
CSS3
JavaScript (ES6)
DOM Manipulation
Local Storage
Session Storagel
Web Crypto API (SHA-256)
Challenges and Learning —
Throughout the internship, I faced several challenges while implementing different features and debugging JavaScript code. Solving these problems improved my analytical thinking and strengthened my confidence in web development. I also learned the importance of writing clean, organized, and reusable code.
Overall Experience —
The OIB-SIP internship was a valuable learning experience. The projects were practical and helped me apply theoretical concepts to real-world applications. I enhanced my problem-solving abilities, improved my coding practices, and gained hands-on experience in building responsive web applications.
I would like to thank Oasis Infobyte for providing this excellent learning opportunity. This internship has motivated me to continue learning modern web technologies and work on more advanced projects in the future.
#OasisInfobyte #OIBSIP #WebDevelopment #HTML #CSS #JavaScript #Internship #FrontendDevelopment
